concerns.text: The plan cites 'LoopCoder-v2 Model' with 'NO verified source found' and assumes the checkpoint is accessible via a standard HuggingFace search. The research question specifically requires an *iterative refinement* model (LoopCoder-v2) to measure convergence trajectories across loops. If this specific checkpoint is not publicly available or the URL is invalid, the core methodology (FR-002, FR-003) cannot be executed as written. The plan must either cite a verified, accessible URL for the specific LoopCoder-v2 checkpoint or explicitly pivot to a verified open-weight iterative model (e.g., a specific CodeLlama variant with known iterative capabilities) before proceeding. Relying on a 'search' for a non-verified artifact makes the study infeasible.

The plan assumes CPU-only inference for a 2B model (LoopCoder-v2-2B) on a GitHub Actions runner (2 vCPU, 7GB RAM) is feasible within 6 hours. A 2B parameter model in FP32 requires ~8GB+ RAM just for weights, exceeding the 7GB limit and causing swapping or OOM. Even if quantized (which the plan explicitly forbids), CPU inference for 164+164 problems with N=10 samples and k=3 loops is computationally prohibitive on 2 vCPUs within the stated 6-hour window. The compute assumption is infeasible; the plan must either secure GPU access, reduce the model size significantly (e.g., <1B), or drastically reduce the sample size ($N$) and loop counts, which may invalidate the statistical power of the correlation analysis.
